title: Rewriting the Rules: The SEC & CFTC on Crypto, IPOs & the Future of American Markets
description:
(0:00) Jason and Chamath welcome SEC's Paul Atkins and CFTC's Michael Selig

(0:53) Atkins on how US markets have changed over his 40 year career

(3:04) Top priorities across both agencies: Fixing the IPO drought, crypto regulation, cutting unnecessary rules

(8:16) AI trading bots, autonomous hedge funds, and investing with leverage

(15:30) Ending the "Turf War" between the SEC and CFTC, super app vision

(19:15) Prediction markets, insider trading, gray area

(26:56) Trump advocates for changing quarterly earnings to bi-annual

(30:30) Changing the accreditation rules a priority for 2026

(34:56) HFT firms that dominate the futures markets, swap reporting

(40:36) VC fund formation

(46:18) US markets vs the world, crypto classification

(52:54) Biggest risks: Market manipulation, crypto scams, and the Gen Z gambling crisis

analysis.summary: The All-In Podcast discusses the future of American markets with SEC's Paul Atkins and CFTC's Michael Selig, covering topics such as crypto regulation, IPO drought, and AI trading bots. They also touch on the 'Turf War' between the SEC and CFTC, prediction markets, and the biggest risks facing the markets. The conversation highlights the need for regulatory clarity and cooperation to foster innovation and growth.

takeaways.text: IPO drought needs to be addressed
Crypto regulation is a top priority
AI trading bots are changing the market landscape
Ending the 'Turf War' between SEC and CFTC is crucial
Market manipulation and crypto scams are significant risks
Changing accreditation rules is a priority for 2026
US markets need to adapt to compete with the world

sentiment.notes: The conversation highlights the need for regulatory clarity and cooperation, but also acknowledges the significant risks facing the markets, resulting in a cautious tone.

notable_moments.description: Discussion on ending the 'Turf War' between SEC and CFTC
Conversation on the biggest risks facing the markets
